reasoning: The Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) successfully recaptured Khartoum and surrounding areas (including Omdurman and Khartoum North) by mid-2025, consolidating full control over Khartoum state by May 2025. Following this, the RSF was pushed out of the central regions, including Al-Jazirah and Sennar, and forced to retreat toward the western regions of Kordofan and Darfur. Recent reporting from late 2025 demonstrates the RSF is focused on consolidating power in the west (such as capturing towns in West Kordofan like Babanusa) and establishing a rival government, rather than launching an offensive back toward the capital. Given the geographical distances involved, the entrenched position of the SAF, the short time horizon (less than three months to March 31, 2026), and the complete lack of RSF presence near Khartoum, a successful RSF offensive to retake the heavily fortified capital is virtually impossible. The community forecast of 2% accurately accounts for these operational realities, and I align with this low probability.
